Name: Beyond Tomorrow Scholars Program
Eligibility: Self-identify as Black; Canadian citizen/permanent resident; strong academic record; financial need; entering UBC undergraduate program directly from high school or transferring
How_to_apply: Submit UBC application; complete Entrance Award Application; Student Application Form, Parent/Guardian Form, Reference Form

Name: Loran Award
Eligibility: First-time university students entering September 2026; Canadian citizen or permanent resident
How_to_apply: Online application; upload transcript; confirm referees

Name: Schulich Leader Scholarships
Field_of_study: STEM; Technology, Engineering, Entrepreneurship, Science, or Applied scientific research
Eligibility: Canadian Citizen, nominated by your high school, Be entrepreneurial-minded, exhibiting leadership, charisma, and creativity
How_to_apply: Must apply to Faculty to STEM program

Name: UBC Centennial Scholars Entrance Awards
Eligibility: Strong academic student, meet UBC admissions requirements, Canadian citizen, permanent resident, or protected person in Canada, entering directly from high school or transferring from another post-secondary institution,  must apply to a direct entry undergraduate program at UBC, and not have a previous degree.
How_to_apply: Submit your UBC application by its entrance deadline.Complete the UBC Entrance Award Application by UBC entrance deadline. Student Application FormDescription of Circumstance (maximum 1,000 words), Parent/Guardian Form, Reference Form

Name: Uggla Family Scholarship - SFU
Eligibility: Available to domestic (Canadian) students admitted for the fall term to any undergraduate degree. This may include students who identify as Indigenous, Black, or a person of colour, students living with disabilities, LGBTQIA2S+ students, and students of all gender identities. The intent of the Uggla Family Scholarship is to promote equity and remove historical and existing discrimination, including barriers to full participation in social, cultural, economic and political life.
How_to_apply: Financial need assessment (family income disclosure)A written statement (250 words max) about your academic potential and goalsAn explanation of your achievements and contributions (250 words max)A short essay sharing your future goals and how these goals have been informed by your personal experiences (750 words)Letters of support

Name: Entrance Scholarship - SFU
Eligibility: Priority to residence, If you’re a current high school student who demonstrates excellence in academic and extracurricular activities, you may be eligible for this scholarship in fall term
How_to_apply: Community Involvement & Achievements listed and reflected on, Essay (500 words), References (academic and community involvement and achievements reference)

Name: BC First Nation Scholarships and Bursaries
Eligibility: Must be applying to a technology program, First Nation (status or non-status) member of a B.C. First Nation Community
How_to_apply: Apply during annual intake (May 13 - August 1)

Name: Nursing Scholarships and Bursaries
Eligibility: Metis or inuit, Must be applying to a nursing program full-time
How_to_apply: Apply during annual intake (May 13 - August 1)

Name: Post-Secondary Student Support Program (PSSSP)
Eligibility: First Nations students registered under the Indian Act, afflicted with a BC band or on BC general list without band membership; must be eligibility requirements
How_to_apply: Submit completed application form, status card, proof of acceptance/enrollment, tuition/books/fees, verification of dependant, successful applicants must submit proof of registration and official transcripts

Name: First Citizens Fund Student Bursary Program
Eligibility: Indigenous students, permanent BC residents, enrolled in recognized post-secondary institution in BC
How_to_apply: Submit completed application form, Status Card (or supporting evidence for non-status), Proof of Registration/Enrollment, Tuition/Books/Fees documentation, Unofficial Transcripts/Grades, Letter of Recommendation, Personal Letter; submit Official Transcripts if successful; currently paused

Name: Encore “Make Your Moment” Bursary
Field_of_study: Hospitality, Event Planning, Film Production, Broadcasting, Audio Engineering
Eligibility: Indigenous ancestry (Status, Non-status, Métis, Inuit); Permanent resident of BC; Registered at recognized Canadian post-secondary institution (priority to BC); Age 18-24; Not repeating a course/academic year
How_to_apply: Submit completed & signed application form, checklist, budget, Indigenous ancestry docs, proof of registration, tuition/books/fees docs, letter of recommendation, personal letter, graduate supervisor letter if applicable; digital submission preferred to education@bcaafc.com

Name: BC Achievement Scholarship
Eligibility: Graduating BC students with top academic achievement (subset of grades 10–12); must meet Dogwood Diploma requirements; ≤1 Transfer Standing (TS) or Standing Granted (SG); Language Arts 12 score ≥ B (73%)
How_to_apply: No application needed; eligible students are automatically considered based on grades; vouchers mailed in November; valid for 5 years

Name: District/Authority Scholarship
Field_of_study: Any (based on student’s area of excellence: Applied Design/Skills/Tech, Community Service, Fine Arts, Indigenous Languages/Culture, Languages, Physical Activity/Health, Technical/Trades Training)
Eligibility: Graduating BC students meeting Dogwood/Adult Dogwood Diploma requirements; meet criteria determined by local scholarship committee; demonstrate outstanding achievement in eligible area of interest
How_to_apply: Students don’t apply directly; contact school or district office (public schools) or FISA (independent schools) for details; vouchers mailed in November; valid for 5 years

Name: BC Excellence Scholarship
Field_of_study: Any (based on chosen career/area of post-secondary study)High School / Graduating StudentsMinimum “B” average in grades 11–12 courses; at least B (73%) in Language Arts 12; no more than one C+ (67%) in Science 11/12, Math 11, Social Studies 11/12
Eligibility: Graduating BC students; meet Dogwood Diploma & BC Graduation Program requirements; demonstrate service & leadership at school and in community; aptitude & commitment to chosen career/field
How_to_apply: Complete online application in one session; nomination form signed by school; written statement; resume; two reference letters; submit by deadline

Name: Pathway to Teacher Education Scholarship
Field_of_study: BCK–12 Teaching / EducationHigh School / Graduating StudentsMinimum “B” average in grades 11–12 courses; at least B (73%) in Language Arts 12; no more than one C+ (67%) in Science 11/12, Math 11, Social Studies 11/12
Eligibility: Graduating BC students; meet Dogwood Diploma & BC Graduation Program requirements; demonstrated commitment & aptitude for K–12 teaching; only one award between this and BC Excellence scholarship
How_to_apply: Complete online application in one session; written statement; mandatory resume; two reference letters; submit by deadline

Name: BCIT Entrance Awards
Eligibility: Canadian citizen or permanent resident; full-time students (≥60% course load); new to BCIT or starting a new program; program ≥5 months duration; meet award-specific criteria
How_to_apply: Apply online via BCIT Entrance Awards portal; one application per calendar year; follow award-specific eligibility in Entrance Awards guide; submit before deadline

Name: BCIT Full-Time Student Bursary
Deadline: Fall: Oct 30, 2026; Winter: Jan 30, 2027; Spring/Summer: Jun 30, 2027
Field_of_study: Eligible Technology, Trades, and Nursing programs
Eligibility: Canadian citizen or permanent resident; full-time student (≥60% course load; 40%+ for approved disabilities); completed first term/level or 1 month of study; enrolled in bursary-eligible program and level
How_to_apply: Apply online via myBCIT: Online Self-Service → Student Financial Aid & Awards → Web Application → select “Bursary – Online Application” → submit by deadline; must click “Mark Application as Complete” to finalize

Name: BCIT Academic Awards
Amount: Varies; based on program and achievement
Eligibility: Full-time BCIT students (100% course load); meet program-specific academic criteria
How_to_apply: No application required; awarded automatically based on academic performance

Name: B.C. Access Grant (Full-Time)
Amount: Up to $4,000 per 8-month year (<2yr programs), $1,000 per 8-month year (≥2yr programs)
Field_of_study: Undergraduate programs at BC public post-secondary institutions
Eligibility: Full-time, assessed financial need, attend B.C. public post-secondary institution
How_to_apply: Automatically assessed when applying for a StudentAid BC loan

Name: B.C. Access Grant (Part-Time)
Field_of_study: Undergraduate programs at BC public post-secondary institutions
Eligibility: Part-time, assessed financial need, attend B.C. public post-secondary institution
How_to_apply: Automatically assessed when applying for part-time StudentAid BC loan

Name: B.C. Access Grant for Deaf Students
Amount: Not specified (based on assessed financial need)
Field_of_study: Full-time programs at specialized institutions for deaf students
Eligibility: Deaf or hard of hearing, enrolled full-time (≥60%), attending eligible institutions (Gallaudet University, National Technical Institute for the Deaf)
How_to_apply: Complete Appendix 8 for Full-Time Students – Disability Programs; Section 4 completed by qualified medical assessor; submit audiogram and documentation to StudentAid BC

Name: B.C. Access Grant for Students with Disabilities
Amount: Up to $1,560 per program year ($30/week of study)
Deadline: None (assessed automatically with student aid application)
Field_of_study: Any full-time program at a designated public or private post-secondary institution
Eligibility: Permanent or persistent/prolonged disability, enrolled full-time institutions (Gallaudet University, National Technical Institute for the Deaf)
How_to_apply: Automatically assessed if disability is verified; otherwise, submit Appendix 8 – Full-Time Students Disability Programs to StudentAid BC

Name: B.C. Scholarship Society Programs
Eligibility: 3 main programs: Indigenous Students Awards, International Scholarships, Transfer Scholarships; must meet program-specific requirements
How_to_apply: Check website for program-specific application instructions

Name: B.C. Supplemental Bursary for Students with Disabilities
Amount: $800 per program year (≥40% course load), $400 per program year (20–39% course load)
Eligibility: Must qualify for Canada Student Grant for Students with Disabilities, Part-time or full-time students with a permanent or persistent/prolonged disability, enrolled in a designated B.C. post-secondary institution
How_to_apply: Automatic assessment if disability is verified with StudentAid BC; otherwise complete Appendix 8 – Disability Programs form

Name: Canada Student Grant for Part-Time Students with Dependants
Amount: Up to $40 per week (2 children) / $60 per week (3+ children); up to $1,920 / $2,688 per school year; temporarily increased 40% until July 31, 2026
Eligibility: Part-time students with dependants under 12 (or older with permanent disability) at designated post-secondary institutions
How_to_apply: Automatic assessment when you apply for part-time student aid

Name: Canada Student Grant for Part-Time Studies
Amount: Up to $1,800 per program year; temporarily increased 40% until July 31, 2026
Eligibility: Low-income part-time students at designated post-secondary institutions
How_to_apply: Automatic assessment when you apply for part-time student aid

Name: Health Program Bursaries – Indigenous Student Recruitment Bursary
Amount: $5,000 per program year (once per 12 months; Sept 1, 2023 – Oct 1, 2025 funding window)
Field_of_study: Priority Health Programs (Dietitian, Midwifery, Nurse Practitioner, Occupational Therapy, Physical Therapy, Practical Nursing, Psychiatric Nursing, Speech Language Pathology)
Eligibility: Must self-identify as First Nations, Inuit, or Métis; Canadian citizen or permanent resident; enrolled in eligible program at a public B.C. institution; not receiving certain other ministry bursaries or employer-sponsored tuition programs
How_to_apply: Automatically assessed by institution’s financial aid office; tuition credit applied

Name: Health Program Bursaries – Student Recruitment and Retention Bursary
Amount: $2,000 per program year (once per 12 months; Sept 1, 2023 – Oct 1, 2025 funding window)
Field_of_study: Priority Health Programs (Advanced Care Paramedic, Primary Care Paramedic, Biomedical Engineering Technologist, Cardiovascular Perfusion, Clinical Counsellor, Environmental Health, Medical Lab Assistant, Medical Lab Technologist, Medical Radiography Technician, MRI Technician – First Discipline, Nuclear Medicine, Radiation Therapy, Rehabilitation Assistant, Respiratory Therapy)
Eligibility: Canadian citizen or permanent resident; enrolled in eligible program at a public B.C. institution; part-time eligible (amount prorated); not eligible if tuition paid by third party (e.g., employer/government program)
How_to_apply: Automatically assessed by institution’s financial aid office; tuition credit applied

Name: Learning Disability Assessment Bursary
Eligibility: Enrolled at designated public B.C. post-secondary institution; approved for StudentAid BC funding; recommended for LD assessment by school accessibility services
How_to_apply: Contact accessibility services office at your post-secondary institution

Name: Learning for Future Grant
Deadline: No fixed deadline (apply through school when enrolled)
Eligibility: Must qualify for Provincial Tuition Waiver Program; enrolled in credential program below graduate level; study period ≥6 weeks or 35 tuition hours
How_to_apply: Submit Declaration Form annually through school Financial Aid Office; first-time applicants submit Tuition Waiver + Grant Application via Financial Aid Office

Name: Nursing Tuition Grant
Deadline: Program must be attended between Sept 1, 2023 – Aug 1, 2026
Eligibility: Canadian citizen or permanent resident; enrolled in eligible nursing program at a B.C. public post-secondary institution (Practical Nursing, BSN, Psychiatric Nursing degrees)
How_to_apply: No application — automatically assessed; tuition credit applied by school

Name: Nursing Tuition Grant – Indigenous
Deadline: Program must be attended between Sept 1, 2023 – Aug 1, 2026
Eligibility: Self-declared First Nations, Métis, or Inuit student; Canadian citizen or permanent resident; enrolled in eligible Bachelor of Science in Nursing program at a B.C. public post-secondary institution (including advanced entry & LPN-to-BSN)
How_to_apply: No application — automatically assessed; tuition credit applied by school

Name: Provincial Tuition Waiver Program
Amount: 100% tuition + eligible fees waived (plus $3,500/yr Learning for Future Grant if eligible)No fixed deadline (apply through school when enrolling)
Deadline: Program must be attended between Sept 1, 2023 – Aug 1, 2026
Eligibility: Current or former youth in care in B.C.; studying below graduate level at eligible B.C. public post-secondary, Native Education College, or approved union trades provider; specific care-status rules apply
How_to_apply: Apply through your post-secondary Financial Aid Office using PTWP + Learning for Future Grant application form

Name: The Nurses Education Bursary
Amount: 100% tuition + eligible fees waived (plus $3,500/yr Learning for Future Grant if eligible)
Deadline: No fixed deadline (apply through school when enrolling)
Eligibility: Enrolled full-time in eligible nursing program ≥4 months; not in default or bankrupt on B.C. student loans
How_to_apply: Auto-assessed when applying for full-time StudentAid BC loan; no separate application required

Name: Youth Futures Education Fund
Amount: Determined case-by-case based on submitted expenses
Eligibility: Must be a recipient of the Former Youth in Care Provincial Tuition Waiver, enrolled in a post-secondary program, in good standing
How_to_apply: Contact Financial Aid & Awards office at your B.C. post-secondary institution, submit completed application form and supporting documentation
